content:
- Executed task 03 (contributors deep dive) from NOW EXECUTE: rewrote all contributors chapters into paragraph-first implementation deep dives; added new chapter docs/src/contributors/api-debug-contracts.md; updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md ordering and ensured contributors/index.md reading order matches SUMMARY.
- Ran all required gates with no skipping: make docs-lint, make docs-build, make docs-hygiene, make check, make test, make lint, make test-long.
- make test initially failed: logging::postgres_ingest::tests::real_binary::ingests_jsonlog_and_stderr_files_from_real_postgres flaked because Postgres accepted TCP but still returned FATAL "database system is starting up" to psql. Fixed by adding a bounded retry helper run_psql_query_with_retry (treating starting-up/not-yet-accepting/connection-refused as transient) in src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs; re-ran make test successfully.
- Marked task file status done/passes true and checked all boxes; ran .ralph/task_switch.sh (updated current_tasks and task_history).
- Next (after pushing): consider adding a bug task to remove/replace src/test_harness/mod.rs #![allow(dead_code)] and any other lingering lint-allow exceptions (per AGENTS.md rule), but not part of task 03 scope.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md to select next task.
- No bug tasks are failing (<passes>false</passes> / <passing>false</passing> not found under bugs); found recurring meta-task with <passes>meta-task</passes>.
- Wrote selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md

- Inspected .ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs/ tasks 01..04 and extracted current status + scope.
- Found that Task 02 already operationalized claim inventory + verification matrix + subagent slicing with fail-closed checks and concrete evidence artifacts under .ralph/evidence/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark/.
- Located pass-6 draft plan in .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md around lines 514+ and identified insertion point between sections 5 and 6.
- Read Task 02 helper scripts (build_scope_map.py, build_claim_inventory.py, build_verification_matrix.py, slice_claims.py, check_slice_coverage.py, merge_subagent_outputs.py, refresh_claim_anchors.py) to ensure proposed workflow is executable and fail-closed.
- Next (if asked) I can patch the pass-6 plan file directly with a new section for claim inventory/evidence mapping and renumber downstream sections.

- Reviewed meta-task requirements in task 18 and compared against pass-6 draft section ending in TO BE VERIFIED.
- Identified hard requirement gaps: no mandatory deletion/archiving of old meta-18 evidence dirs; no explicit review of all story-operator-architecture-docs tasks; missing full non-trivial doc-claim inventory with path:line and evidence method; missing explicit >=15 parallel subagents for claim verification; missing ownership/pass-uncertain-risk artifacts; missing explicit ban on claim checklist UI in docs; missing explicit usability/config-centralization/auth-variant coverage steps from acceptance criteria.
- Also flagged weak spots: real-binary section checks existence/install but not execution provenance proof; no explicit requirement to assess test gaps by production impact before touching tests.
- Prepared concrete checkbox additions grouped by insertion section for pass-6 plan.
- Next: provide user with precise file-line-backed gap list and paste-ready checkbox edits.

- Ran startup-mandated progress read via .ralph/progress_read.sh.
- Performed broad repo scans with rg for lint bypass/ignore markers and forbidden error-handling shortcuts (unwrap/expect/panic/todo/unimplemented).
- Broad scan hotspots were mostly noise from .ralph task/progress text and docs/mermaid.min.js (vendor/minified JS contains .expect().
- Narrowed to active code paths (src/tests/examples/scripts + key top-level files) with exclusions for .ralph, target, docs/book, docs/mermaid.min.js.
- Findings in code paths: zero forbidden shortcut tokens in .rs files; zero #[ignore]/clippy allow/expect suppressions; seven generic lint bypasses as #![allow(dead_code)] in src/{api,process,pginfo,dcs,debug_api,ha,test_harness}/mod.rs.
- Pulled pass-6 draft plan section to align naming; plan already references track-a-forbidden-scan.txt.
- Should do next after quitting due to context: if requested, patch pass-6 plan with exact rg commands and explicit evidence file names (track-a/b/c), and decide whether dead_code allows are acceptable or should be removed with bug/task.

- Audited Makefile targets make check/test/test-long/lint with line-numbered inspection and make -n expansion.
- Read tools/docs-mermaid-lint.mjs and tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh because lint invokes both.
- Confirmed timeout usage exists only in make test execution phase; preflight list and other gates are unbounded.
- Identified silent-pass/false-confidence risks: docs no-code guard only scans selected roots + only column-1 backtick fences; test-long logs many 0-test binaries and has no bounded watchdog.
- Sampled existing .ralph/evidence logs and confirmed they lack normalized per-step duration/exit/timeout metadata.
- Created grouped bug task: .ralph/tasks/bugs/gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md to track fixes.
- Next if continuing: implement a shared timeout+evidence wrapper for all gate phases, tighten docs guard coverage, and add explicit per-test execution assertions/timeout diagnostics.

- Read previous progress log and confirmed current task is `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Found pass-6 plan draft ended with `TO BE VERIFIED`; performed deep skeptical verification using 4 explorer subagents in parallel.
- Updated pass-6 plan to close mandatory gaps from the meta-task contract:
  - Added explicit pre-run purge of old `.ralph/evidence/meta-18-*` dirs and guard that `<passes>meta-task</passes>` stays unchanged.
  - Added mandatory operator-doc claim verification workflow (read all operator-doc tasks every run; claim inventory + evidence mapping; 15+ parallel slices; fail-closed coverage checks; artifacts written under evidence).
  - Strengthened repo scans to also treat any `#![allow(...)]` as a failure (not just clippy-specific ignores) and added a more structured scan kit with evidence filenames.
  - Added real-binary provenance proof requirements (sha256/file/readlink + strace execve proof + negative-control stub swap).
  - Added usability/config-centralization/auth-variant coverage checklist items.
  - Added “test gaps must be tied to production/operator impact” and “don’t loosen assertions first” constraints.
  - Clarified `EVID` evidence-dir shell variable usage.
- Replaced the pass-6 plan terminator with `NOW EXECUTE` (so next pickup should execute without further exploration).
- Noted subagent findings:
  - Repo currently contains `#![allow(dead_code)]` in multiple `src/*/mod.rs` roots; plan now requires removing these (no lint exceptions).
  - Gate audit found unbounded hang risks (esp `make test-long`); a new bug task file was created by the subagent: `.ralph/tasks/bugs/gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md`.

- Next: execute the pass-6 plan from `NOW EXECUTE` (fresh evidence dir, docs claim verification, scan/fix lint-bypass, gate hardening if needed, then run `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, `make test-long`).

- Inspected all four task files under .ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs with line-numbered reads.
- Extracted strongest behavioral/safety verification claims primarily from Task 02 (verification matrix rules, DCS reconnect/reset semantics, API route/auth ownership, fencing boundedness) and supporting constraints from Tasks 01/03/04.
- Noted key process inconsistency: Task 01 is marked done/passes true while acceptance checklist remains entirely unchecked.
- Noted future-risk gap: Task 04 is not started and does not explicitly require re-running Task 02 claim inventory/matrix pipeline after deep doc expansions.
- Next if continuing: produce a concrete “claim inventory v2” template CSV and an automated validator command sequence to enforce no-miss/no-dup/no-unverified/no-unsliced claims.

- Ran startup-required .ralph/progress_read.sh and then performed targeted repo scans with rg for lint bypass markers and forbidden tokens.
- Scope used for actionable findings: code paths (src/tests/examples/scripts + top-level build files), excluding .ralph/task logs, target/, docs/book/, and vendor docs/mermaid.min.js noise.
- Findings:
  - Seven module-level lint bypasses remain: #![allow(dead_code)] in src/api/mod.rs, src/process/mod.rs, src/pginfo/mod.rs, src/dcs/mod.rs, src/debug_api/mod.rs, src/ha/mod.rs, src/test_harness/mod.rs.
  - No #[allow(clippy::...)] or #[expect(clippy::...)] found in Rust code.
  - No direct forbidden callsites found in Rust code for .unwrap(...), .expect(...), panic!(...), todo!(...), unimplemented!(...).
  - clippy restriction posture is present and strict in src/lib.rs plus Makefile lint targets.
  - cfg(test) harness patterns are dense (74 in src, 25 in src/test_harness) with key gates at src/lib.rs, src/ha/mod.rs, src/test_harness/mod.rs, and src/state/errors.rs.
- Next if continuing from this point: remove module-root #![allow(dead_code)] incrementally (start with test_harness + ha), gate test-only bits with #[cfg(test)]/feature flags at item level, run make lint/check/test after each module batch, and add a fail-closed grep guard in CI for allow attributes and forbidden tokens.

- Audited real-binary installer + harness trust chain end-to-end: tools/install-postgres16.sh, tools/install-etcd.sh, src/test_harness/binaries.rs, plus call-sites in ha_e2e/startup.rs, test_harness/{pg16,etcd3}.rs, and process/worker command construction.
- Confirmed current workspace state: .tools/postgres16/bin/* are symlinks to /usr/bin/* (PostgreSQL 16.11); .tools/etcd/bin/etcd is a regular executable (v3.6.8); cached archive sha matches pinned installer checksum cf9cfe...ea693.
- Identified trust gaps: harness only enforces exists/regular/executable (no canonical/owner/hash/version policy), symlinks accepted implicitly via fs::metadata, installer command PATH trust, postgres installer links mutable system binaries without in-repo provenance manifest/signature pin.
- Added grouped bug task for provenance hardening: .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-real-binary-provenance-enforcement-gaps.md.
- Next if continuing: implement a fail-closed verify-real-binaries gate script + harness canonicalization/hash/version checks and add strace/negative-control CI evidence automation.

- Reviewed current Makefile gate targets (check/test/test-long/lint) and helper scripts tools/docs-mermaid-lint.mjs + tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh with line-numbered inspection.
- Validated dry-run expansions (`make -n`) for all gate targets and confirmed timeout coverage gaps: test preflight unbounded, test-long unbounded, check/lint unbounded.
- Demonstrated Make-variable bypass vectors with reproducible dry-runs:
  - `make -n TIMEOUT_BIN=true test` shows execution replaced by `true ... cargo test` (tests not run).
  - `make -n ULTRA_LONG_SKIP_ARGS='--exact not_a_real_test_name' test` shows final command can be forced to a 0-test filter.
  - `make -n TEST_TIMEOUT_SECS=999999 ... test` shows timeout can be effectively disabled.
- Confirmed ULTRA_LONG_TESTS external override is blocked (`make -n ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=foo test` fails as intended).
- Audited optional skip posture in harness: real-binary requirements are fail-closed via require_*_for_real_tests (no env-gated early return path found).
- Attempted live `cargo test --all-targets -- --list` count capture; run failed due known mount linker/archive flake (`failed to build archive ... No such file or directory`), so review evidence is static/dry-run + code inspection.
- Noted existing bug tracker file `.ralph/tasks/bugs/gate-audit-timeout-silent-pass-hardening.md` already captures most timeout/evidence issues; current review adds env-var/make-var bypass and ignored/0-test pass risks.
- Next if continuing: implement hardening in Makefile/scripts (lock override-sensitive vars, add bounded wrappers for all phases, enforce executed-test assertions, widen docs guard coverage) and collect structured gate evidence artifacts.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Spawned 4 explorer subagents in parallel:
  - operator-doc task inventory + claim-verification schema/slicing suggestions,
  - lint-bypass / allow-attribute scan results,
  - Makefile gate realism/silent-pass risks,
  - real-binary installer/harness provenance gaps.
- Updated the task file to include a more detailed pass-6 plan incorporating subagent findings:
  - added stricter operator-doc claim inventory schema (freeze_commit, polarity/modality, fail_criteria, etc.)
  - added explicit scans and special focus list of existing `#![allow(dead_code)]` module roots to remove
  - added explicit Makefile bypass vectors to harden (TIMEOUT_BIN / ULTRA_LONG_SKIP_ARGS / timeouts)
  - added provenance steps and cross-reference to existing real-binary provenance bug.
- Replaced all `NOW EXECUTE` markers in the task file (historical pass marker renamed; pass-6 ends with `TO BE VERIFIED`).
- Next: per task protocol, do a deep skeptical verification of this plan (must alter at least one thing), then switch terminator to `NOW EXECUTE` and execute the plan end-to-end with all required gates.

- Ran startup progress read, then executed repo-wide Rust scans for lint suppression markers: crate/item-level allow/expect/ignore attributes, plus pass-6 regex-equivalent Track B/C/D queries.
- Findings are unchanged from prior snapshot: exactly seven `#![allow(dead_code)]` entries at module roots (`src/api/mod.rs`, `src/process/mod.rs`, `src/pginfo/mod.rs`, `src/dcs/mod.rs`, `src/debug_api/mod.rs`, `src/ha/mod.rs`, `src/test_harness/mod.rs`); no `#[allow(...)]` item attributes, no `#[expect(...)]`/`#![expect(...)]`, no `#[ignore]`/`#[ignore = ...]` in Rust code.
- Validated pass-6 special-focus list in task 18 matches live scan exactly (no files only-in-plan or only-in-scan).
- Noted plan-hardening opportunities (no code edits made): broaden scan scope to all tracked `*.rs` (future-proof beyond hardcoded dirs), add explicit generic `expect` attribute sweep (non-clippy), and add an equality check between expected special-focus list and Track C unique file set to fail closed on drift.
- Next if continuing: patch task-18 pass-6 section with those scan command refinements and optional auto-generated expected-list comparison artifact.

- Audited Makefile and gate scripts for real-binary enforcement and timeout/bypass vectors requested by user.
- Confirmed real-binary enforcement is no longer env-var-driven: `PGTUSKMASTER_REQUIRE_REAL_BINARIES` does not appear in runtime code/Makefile; enforcement is via `require_*_for_real_tests` in src/test_harness/binaries.rs, with representative hard-fail call path in ha_e2e/startup.rs.
- Confirmed `make test-long` has no outer timeout and no ensure-timeout dependency; all `cargo test` calls in that target are unbounded.
- Reproduced bypass vectors with dry-run evidence:
  - `TIMEOUT_BIN=true` rewrites wrapper to `true ... cargo test`, enabling full skip.
  - `ULTRA_LONG_SKIP_ARGS='--exact not_a_real_test_name'` rewrites make test execution to a 0-test filter.
  - `TEST_TIMEOUT_SECS=999999 TEST_TIMEOUT_KILL_AFTER_SECS=999999` effectively disables test timeout guard.
- Verified one guard already fail-closed: `ULTRA_LONG_TESTS` external override is blocked at parse-time (`Makefile:7`).
- Prepared recommendations to tighten pass-6 plan: add mandatory negative-control commands proving override lockout, add explicit stale-env-var claim cleanup, require timeout bounds for every gate phase, and codify Makefile origin-guard hardening plus `test-long: ensure-timeout`.
- Next if continuing: patch pass-6 plan checkboxes and/or Makefile with override-origin guards and bounded wrappers, then capture before/after evidence artifacts under EVID.

- Audited current operator-doc task set (.ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs/01..04) and pass-6 plan section 1.5 in meta task 18.
- Confirmed status inconsistency: task 01 header says done/passes true while acceptance checklist has 20 unchecked boxes; task 04 is not_started with 43 unchecked acceptance bullets (future contractual claims).
- Skimmed docs + task claim language patterns and compared against pass-6 candidate regex. Found major coverage gap: pass-6 regex misses many normative patterns (should/only/required/bounded/expected/leader/primary/replica/initdb etc). Quick diff showed ~350 line anchors matched by extended modal set but not by current pass-6 token set.
- Reviewed prior task-02 claim tooling scripts in evidence dir; they validate duplicate/missing claim IDs and slice coverage, but pass-6 checklist currently lacks explicit fail-closed checks for candidate-to-inventory adjudication, verified-without-evidence anchors, source-path/line validity, and scope-map generation inputs.
- Should do next if continuing: patch pass-6 section 1.5 with explicit source additions (SUMMARY + verification ledgers + prior task-02 artifacts + scope map inputs), add schema columns (source_kind/scope_status/candidate_anchor/adjudication_reason/evidence_kind_actual), and add mechanical fail-closed checks (candidate adjudication 1:1, verified rows require non-doc evidence anchors, absolute claims require mechanical guard/test, and task status-vs-checkbox parity checks).

- Reviewed current provenance plan section in task 18 plus installer scripts and pass-5 provenance evidence artifacts.
- Confirmed installer behavior: install-postgres16 symlinks .tools binaries to trusted system paths (/usr/.../bin) and install-etcd is pinned + checksum-verified.
- Found key provenance gap: plan/evidence expect .tools/postgres16/bin/postgres execve hit, but strace evidence shows hit=no for that path while postgres is actually execve'd as /usr/bin/postgres via pg_ctl internals; this means the current assertion is mis-specified and not fail-closed in practice.
- Found negative-control safety/repeatability gap: stub-swap evidence only proves test failure + marker existence; no auditable restore verification (post-restore hash/version), no stale-marker guard requirement, and no explicit lock/isolation around mutating shared .tools/etcd/bin/etcd.
- Should do next: tighten plan to canonical-path-aware strace assertions (tool path + resolved target path), make negative-control script deterministic with unique marker + pre-clean + flock + trap + post-restore integrity check, and optionally avoid global binary mutation by allowing temporary per-run binary-path override.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Detected pass-6 plan ended with `TO BE VERIFIED`, so performed the required deep skeptical verification using 4 parallel explorer subagents (Makefile bypasses, operator-doc claim verification gaps, allow/ignore markers scan, real-binary provenance proof safety).
- Updated the pass-6 plan in `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`:
  - Strengthened operator-doc claim verification to be more fail-closed: added scope-map inputs (`docs-src-files.txt`, `summary-reachable-files.txt`, orphan triage), broadened candidate feeds (modal tokens + checkbox feed), added schema columns for adjudication and evidence-kind, and added additional coverage checks (verified claims require evidence anchors; absolute/negative claims require guard/test/tooling evidence; task header passes must match acceptance checklist or waiver).
  - Tightened repo-wide scans: switched from hardcoded dir list to `git ls-files -z '*.rs' | xargs -0 -r rg ...` and expanded bypass scan to catch generic `#[expect(...)]` (not just clippy). Fixed the earlier accidental `$$?` to `$?` so the “allow exit 1 only” pattern is correct.
  - Added explicit Makefile negative-control evidence steps (`make -n TIMEOUT_BIN=true test`, `ULTRA_LONG_SKIP_ARGS=...`, extreme `TEST_TIMEOUT_SECS`) and made the plan require parse-time failure after hardening.
  - Updated real-binary provenance criteria to be canonical-path aware (wrappers + `readlink -f` targets) and hardened the negative-control etcd stub swap requirements (unique marker, pre/post sha256, flock isolation, trap restore, and failure reason must be early/etcd-specific).
- Replaced the plan terminator with `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Next: execute pass-6 exactly as written (no more exploration). In execution, ensure all gates pass: `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, `make test-long`. Any failures must become bug tasks.

- Reviewed current Makefile test gates with line-level inspection and reproduced bypass vectors via make -n.
- Confirmed concrete bypasses: TIMEOUT_BIN can be overridden to true, ULTRA_LONG_SKIP_ARGS can be overridden to force 0-test filter, and TEST_TIMEOUT_* can be inflated to effectively disable timeout enforcement.
- Confirmed existing fail-closed guard: ULTRA_LONG_TESTS external override is blocked at Makefile parse time.
- Confirmed timeout coverage gaps remain: make test preflight unbounded; make test-long commands unbounded; make check/lint unbounded.
- Pulled AGENTS constraints to include warm-build guidance and evidence-log handling (`cargo test --all-targets --no-run`, .ralph/evidence ignored + git add -f).
- Next if continuing: implement Makefile hardening (origin guards + bounded wrappers + non-zero executed-test assertions), then run negative-control proofs and capture structured evidence logs.

- Ran startup progress_read and then performed a fresh no-Pcre2 repo scan for no-unwrap/no-expect/no-panic and lint-suppression attributes.
- Confirmed via tracked Rust file scans (`git ls-files '*.rs' | xargs rg ...`) that there are 0 hits for `.unwrap(`, `.expect(`, `panic!(`, `unwrap_err`, `expect_err`, `todo!`, `unimplemented!`, `unreachable!`, `panic_any`.
- Confirmed lint suppression in Rust is currently limited to 7 crate-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` attributes in module roots: api/dcs/debug_api/ha/pginfo/process/test_harness mod.rs files; no item-level `#[allow(...)]`, no `#[expect(...)]`, no cfg_attr allow/expect, no `#[ignore]` tests.
- Checked lint policy anchors: `src/lib.rs` denies clippy unwrap/expect/panic/todo/unimplemented and Makefile lint target repeats strict clippy denies for lib/tests/all-targets.
- Should do next: provide user with a fail-closed deep-review checklist script/commands that freeze scan scope, treat rg exit codes rigorously, capture per-pattern evidence files, include positive-control regex sanity checks, and enforce a final non-empty scope + empty-forbidden-results invariant.

- Reviewed provenance enforcement code path requested by user: src/test_harness/binaries.rs, tools/install-*.sh, and HA e2e harness entry points.
- Confirmed enforcement today is existence+regular-file+exec-bit only in require_real_binary; no canonical-target allowlist, owner checks, checksum, or version checks at harness gate.
- Confirmed installers: install-etcd has pinned archive SHA + version verification; install-postgres16 resolves from trusted absolute locations but still creates symlinks to mutable system binaries and only checks major-version strings.
- Traced e2e startup path uses require_*_for_real_tests and passes absolute binary paths through runtime config, but runtime provenance assertions are not encoded in tests themselves.
- Verified prior pass-5 evidence mismatch: required-hit file marks .tools/postgres16/bin/postgres hit=no while strace exec logs contain /usr/bin/postgres (canonical target via pg_ctl), so assertion currently needs canonical-path-aware rules.
- Identified additional PATH-sensitive runtime gap: harness code invokes Command::new("kill") in pg16/etcd3/ha_e2e util. Added focused bug task file: .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-test-harness-runtime-path-dependent-kill-command.md.
- Next: provide user findings-first review with concrete file/line references, stronger proof steps (canonical path + sha256 + strace + negative controls), PATH-usage detection strategy, and fresh-run plan additions.

- Reviewed pass-6 section (line-numbered) in task 18, focused on operator-doc claim verification methodology and evidence artifacts.
- Cross-checked against story-operator task-02 methodology and prior evidence scripts/artifacts.
- Found concrete pass-6 coverage gap: docs file list command uses `git ls-files 'docs/src/**/*.md'` which misses top-level docs (`docs/src/introduction.md`, `docs/src/SUMMARY.md`). Verified by diffing globstar-only vs with `docs/src/*.md`.
- Found concrete claim-candidate gap: pass-6 token feed misses API endpoint claim lines in `docs/src/interfaces/node-api.md` (lines 7,8,9,15,16,17), meaning claim discovery can miss contract statements not carrying selected modal keywords.
- Found false-confidence evidence-anchor gap: pass-6 only requires non-empty evidence anchor, but prior matrix has shorthand `/home/...` anchors that are non-resolvable strings; this can pass checks while not being auditable.
- Found pass-6 severity-balance check is not mechanically enforced despite requirement; historical slice distribution shows many slices with 0 high/critical claims.
- Found operator-doc task status/checklist drift is real now (task 01 header done/passes=true with 20 unchecked boxes), so metadata parity checks should be broader and generated mechanically.
- Drafted pass-7 improvements: multi-feed extraction (lexical+structural), full candidate adjudication ledger, evidence-anchor resolvability checks, freshness/commit pin checks, high-risk dual verification, and machine fail-closed scripts.
- Next: provide user with findings-first review + append-ready pass-7 checklist block ending in TO BE VERIFIED.

- Read previous progress log (progress_read) and confirmed current task is `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and the task file; found existing pass-6 plan ended with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Spawned 4 parallel explorer subagents to do research for a fresh-run plan (operator-doc claim verification hardening; lint/unwrap/panic scans; Makefile silent-pass bypasses; real-binary provenance/PATH leak risks).
- Incorporated subagent findings into a new full fresh-run plan appended as pass-7 and ensured the task file now ends with `TO BE VERIFIED` (per workflow).
  - Key plan changes vs pass-6: docs file enumeration includes `docs/src/*.md` (not just `**/*.md`), multi-feed claim candidate discovery incl endpoints/structure, 1:1 candidate-ledger reconciliation, evidence anchor resolvability/freshness checks, severity-balance mechanical check, operator-doc acceptance parity check, Makefile bypass checks for TIMEOUT_BIN/ULTRA_LONG_SKIP_ARGS/TEST_TIMEOUT*, explicit 0-test prevention, explicit PATH-leak detection + canonical-path-aware strace requirements, treat `#![allow(dead_code)]` as bugs to remove.
- Next: per workflow, do DEEP SKEPTICAL VERIFICATION of the pass-7 plan (must alter at least one thing) and then replace the terminator with `NOW EXECUTE` before executing.

- Reviewed pass-7 plan section 2.* and parity checks with fail-closed focus.
- Found concrete weaknesses: path:line dedupe can silently drop claims, >=15 slices + per-slice high/critical is infeasible and can false-fail, non-recursive operator-doc task discovery risks future misses, and waiver_ref in parity is under-specified (possible bypass).
- Prepared concrete line-level edits for each issue; next handoff should apply these edits directly in the task plan before switching TO BE VERIFIED -> NOW EXECUTE.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md` and confirmed pass-7 ended with `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Per workflow, performed DEEP skeptical verification of the pass-7 plan using 4 parallel explorer subagents (operator-doc claim verification, Makefile bypass/0-test risks, real-binary provenance proof safety, repo-wide scans + fail-open review scope).
- Updated the pass-7 plan in `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md` to address concrete gaps:
  - operator-doc task discovery now includes recursive `**/*.md`
  - claim candidate union is now keyed by `path:line:text_hash` with `candidate_id` to avoid silent multi-claim-on-one-line drops
  - verification slicing is adaptive; removed impossible “each slice has a high/critical” rule and replaced with round-robin distribution + waiver when none exist
  - added deterministic endpoint-feed emptiness guard and file-level coverage gate
  - parity now requires a `waiver-registry.csv` with expiry validation
  - repo scans expanded (unreachable/abort/exit/should_panic) + added clippy deny gates and an error-swallowing sweep
  - Makefile audit now includes `MAKEFLAGS` adversarial checks and post-hardening negative controls (env + cmdline overrides)
  - provenance proof now requires `strace` preflight + lock, stricter manifest (permissions + allowlist), machine-checkable strace parsing, safer etcd negative control (shadow copy + positive-control rerun)
  - deep logic review scope now explicitly includes `src/process/*` and `src/config/*`
- Replaced the plan terminator with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: execute pass-7 exactly as written (no more exploration), capture evidence, harden Makefile as needed, fix any found fail-open/lint issues, and run all mandatory gates (`make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, `make test-long`).

- Started new request: verify S03 doc claims against code/tests and output claim_id|status|evidence|notes.
- Loaded prior progress context from progress_read and now switching to targeted file/line verification for each listed claim.
- Next step: inspect referenced doc lines and trace matching code/tests with precise anchors, then compile a strict verification matrix.

- Verified S07 doc claims against repository code/tests by reading referenced docs and tracing implementation paths.
